source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
The variation with potential difference $V$ of the charge $Q$ on one of the plates of a capacitor is shown in Fig. 5.1.

The capacitor is connected to an 8.0 V power supply and two resistors R and S as shown in Fig. 5.2.
The resistance of R is $25\,\mathrm{k}\Omega$ and the resistance of S is $220\,\mathrm{k}\Omega$.
The switch can be in either position X or position Y.
(b) The switch is now moved to position Y.
 Calculate the time constant of the discharge circuit.

image_dependency: Question explicitly depends on Fig. 5.1 and Fig. 5.2 for the Q-V relationship and the circuit layout.
Input flags `requires_image=true` and `text_dominant=false` indicate the problem is not solvable from text alone.
The needed information includes both a graph and a circuit diagram, which are visual assets in the registry.
multi_step: Solving requires extracting capacitor information from the Q-V graph, not just reading a direct value from text.
The switch position change to Y requires determining the relevant discharge-path resistance from the circuit diagram.
The final answer requires combining at least two inferred quantities through the capacitor time-constant relation.
verifiability: Answer format is a single numeric short text (`3.3 s`), which is straightforward to compare against expected output.
The target quantity is governed by a standard physics formula, so the reasoning path is checkable once figure values are read.
Asset registry passed and both question and answer are present, which supports downstream validation.

A human annotator can understand both the task and the expected numeric answer.
